rule: Every F1523.storeRef() call and every searchDocuments() call reads tenantId from ALS context, never from a function parameter. Only T667 (MarketplaceTranslationCacheJob) is permitted to switch CLS to MASTER_TENANT_ID context; its writes then still obey the rule (ALS.tenantId == MASTER_TENANT_ID).
Only MarketplaceTranslationCacheJob may switch CLS to MASTER_TENANT_ID for translation. Any other service that switches CLS for translation purposes fails this arbiter.

GET /api/translations/:moduleId/:locale returns HTTP 200 in all execution paths. Any error (AI failure, policy denied, CAS failure) produces { fallback: true, locale: 'en' }. No 4xx or 5xx from this endpoint.
Concurrent GET /api/translations for the same tenantId/moduleId/locale produce exactly one CAS blob and one lookup ref. Second writer finds ref already present and returns cached result.

If acceptLanguage header is null, absent, or normalises to null via BCP-47, TranslationRequestRegistrar writes nothing to xiigen-translation-requests and does not mutate i18n.enabledLocales. No-op is correct.

If IAiProvider.generateStructured() returns a payload that fails JSON schema validation, the pipeline falls through to CF-812 fallback and returns { fallback: true, locale: 'en' }. No partial translation is written to CAS. xiigen-translation-refs count is unchanged on this path.

MarketplaceTranslationCacheJob uses SETNX with key idem:translate:master:${moduleId}:${locale} at ORDER 1 (before any AI call). Concurrent job instances for the same moduleId/locale produce exactly one master CAS blob; duplicates return { skipped: true }.
Mandatory scope-isolation arbiter (FC-32) — every service in this flow must be validated against Rule 6 (tenantId from ALS). This record is kept last per the bulk NDJSON convention.
